decisions.reasoning: SMA signal flipped to flat (fast 211.8 < slow 213.9) but fundamental thesis is intact: FY2026 revenue $215.94B (+65% YoY), Apple secured as AI client, Isaac GR00T humanoid robot announced at Computex, analyst consensus Strong Buy with PT $298 vs current $203. June 5 semiconductor wipeout was macro/sentiment-driven not NVDA-specific. Holding through volatility.

decisions.invalidation: NVDA-specific guidance cut, export restriction escalation to China, or competitor GPU catching up materially in data center share

decisions.reasoning: Strong Q3 FY26: EPS $4.27 vs $4.07 estimate, revenue $82.89B +18.3% YoY, Azure +40%, AI at $37B ARR +123% YoY. Analyst consensus 52 Buy / 3 Hold / 0 Sell, PT $561 vs current $393. DoD contract ~$9.69B is near-term positive catalyst. Down 16% YTD due to macro rate re-pricing — no fundamental impairment.

decisions.invalidation: Azure growth decelerates below 30%, FOMC rate hike materially exceeds expectations, or AI capex spend plateaus

decisions.reasoning: WWDC 2026 disappointment on Siri AI already absorbed — stock -2% on June 8. Q2 2026 revenue $111.2B +16.6% YoY is strong. Morgan Stanley raised PT to $360 and Maxim to $350 on June 9, implying 20-24% upside from current $291. Tim Cook final WWDC noted — leadership transition is a medium-term watch item but not a sell catalyst today.

decisions.invalidation: iOS 27 AI features disappoint in adoption, China iPhone sales decline materially, or Tim Cook departure causes disorderly transition

decisions.reasoning: Broad market anchor. Goldman Sachs raised year-end S&P 500 target to 8000, Yardeni to 8250, driven by AI capex boom. SPY SMA signal is long. Caution: only 17% of S&P 500 stocks outperforming the index over past month (narrow breadth), and CPI at 4.2% may compress multiples if Fed hikes.

decisions.invalidation: Fed executes rate hike in 2026, S&P 500 earnings estimates cut more than 10%, or breadth deteriorates below 10%

summary: First session: held all four positions (NVDA, MSFT, AAPL, SPY), no trades placed. Macro headwinds (CPI 4.2%, Fed rate-hike risk, June 5 tech selloff) explain ~7% unrealized losses but fundamentals remain intact. Gross long at 92.6% leaves ~$229 before 95% cap.
open_theses: NVDA: AI infrastructure supercycle intact — Apple client win, humanoid robots, data center demand; holding through macro-driven SMA flat signal
MSFT: Azure +40%, AI at $37B ARR; deep value vs $561 analyst PT at current $393
AAPL: Post-WWDC dip at $291 vs analyst PTs $350-360; Q2 revenue beat underpins floor
SPY: Long signal, Goldman year-end target 8000; watching narrow breadth as key risk indicator
Watch macro: FOMC June/July meeting and July CPI print — rate-hike odds at 42.7% are the primary portfolio risk
